role: A fixed SVG overlay that draws a dynamic ink curve between a selected wheel dot and its detail card so the visual relationship is explicit.
capabilities: Locates the selected dot via [data-tether-point] and the detail card via [data-tether-card]
Computes client rects and anchors the tether to the nearest point on the card border
Offsets the tether start from the dot center by a fixed radius
Bows the curve away from the wheel hub, falling back to a perpendicular bow
Hides the tether when the dot and card are too close
Updates path and opacity each frame via requestAnimationFrame and refs
substance_why: The file implements a complete geometry-aware tether with start offset, directional bow, fallback logic, visibility threshold, and lifecycle cleanup.
interactions_missing: No explicit resize/scroll listeners; relies entirely on the rAF loop
No handling for multiple selected points or multiple tether targets
Hardcoded dot radius (7) and bow limits (130, 0.22, 18)
No robust fallback if the wheel hub element is absent or the card is offscreen
performance_risks: Continuous requestAnimationFrame loop while a point is selected
getBoundingClientRect called twice per frame, potentially forcing layout
SVG attributes set every frame even when opacity is 0 if elements are missing
No throttling or pausing when the tether is offscreen or occluded
reuse_why: The visual concept and geometry are worth keeping, but the DOM querying and per-frame layout reads should be replaced with refs, ResizeObserver, and a throttled or event-driven loop.
decomposition_hints: Split pure geometry (anchor, bow, path) into a testable utility
Split the animation driver into a reusable rAF/observer hook
Separate the dot/card discovery layer from the SVG renderer
